intro: Schenkia elegans, also known as Japanese angelica or Schenkia, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Japan and China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schenkia elegans include sesquiterpenes, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Schenkia elegans include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health.
